name: Camino de Los Ángeles and Cerro de Monte Alto Circular
name_original: Torres de Alameda — ruta circular por el Camino de Los Ángeles y  el Camino del Monte Alto
description: This moderate 6.9-mile hiking route near Torres de Alameda explores the Camino de Los Ángeles and Camino del Monte Alto with scenic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Torres De La Alameda offers accessible opportunities for outdoor activities through its green spaces and notable circular routes. The terrain is characterized by easily accessible paths that lead towards the Cerro de Monte Alto, providing views amidst fields. The municipality also features well-maintained urban parks, offering pleasant areas for walks and relaxation within a historical setting. Best hiking trails in Torres De La Alameda The most popular hiking route is Torres de Alameda — circular route through the Camino de Los Ángeles and the Camino del Monte Alto, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads towards the Cerro de Monte Alto, offering serene surroundings amidst fields. Another top favourite among local hikers is Return to Cerro de Monte Alto and Pico del Búho from Torres de Alameda, an easy 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail features gentle gradients and takes about 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. Local hikers also love the Mirador de los Pájaros — round trip from Torres de Alameda, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Torres De La Alameda is defined by its green spaces, circular routes, and urban parks, offering a network of trails for different ability levels. The routes in Torres De La Alameda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Torres De La Alameda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Torres De La Alameda?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails in and around Torres De La Alameda, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 11 easy routes and 12 moderate options, with no difficult trails, making the area accessible for most hikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Torres De La Alameda?
A: Yes, Torres De La Alameda offers several family-friendly routes, particularly the easy trails. An excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298699332/return-to-cerro-de-monte-alto-and-pico-del-buho-from-torres-de-alameda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Cerro de Monte Alto and Pico del Búho from Torres de Alameda&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.3 km long and features gentle gradients, taking about 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. The urban parks like Parque Olof Palme also provide pleasant, accessible green spaces for shorter walks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Torres De La Alameda?
A: The hiking terrain in Torres De La Alameda is generally characterized by easily accessible paths, often leading through fields and green spaces. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306835650/torres-de-alameda-circular-route-through-the-camino-de-los-angeles-and-the-camino-del-monte-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Los Ángeles and Camino del Monte Alto circular route&lt;/a&gt; offer serene surroundings with moderate elevation changes, providing beautiful views without overly challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Torres De La Alameda?
A: Yes, circular routes are a highlight in Torres De La Alameda. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306835650/torres-de-alameda-circular-route-through-the-camino-de-los-angeles-and-the-camino-del-monte-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torres de Alameda — circular route through the Camino de Los Ángeles and the Camino del Monte Alto&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.1 km long and takes around 3 hours 5 minutes. Another moderate circular trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306932319/loeches-from-torres-de-alba-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loeches from Torres de Alba — circular&lt;/a&gt;, covering 12.7 km.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails offer views towards the &lt;strong&gt;Cerro de Monte Alto&lt;/strong&gt;, providing scenic vistas of the surrounding fields. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306882221/mirador-de-los-pajaros-round-trip-from-torres-de-alameda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Pájaros — round trip from Torres de Alameda&lt;/a&gt; is specifically named for its viewpoints. Additionally, the &lt;strong&gt;Parque del Calvario&lt;/strong&gt;, an urban park, offers unique perspectives of the old town, especially at sunrise, and is built on a mound with archaeological significance.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Torres De La Alameda?
A: The trails in Torres De La Alameda are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained green spaces, the accessible nature of the paths, and the serene surroundings, particularly on routes leading to Cerro de Monte Alto.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the hiking areas?
A: Yes, Torres De La Alameda has a rich history. You can explore nearby historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6286099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls and Antiquarium of Alcalá de Henares&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Parque del Calvario&lt;/strong&gt; within the municipality itself is built on a site with Iron Age archaeological findings, offering a glimpse into the area's ancient past.

Q: Are there any urban green spaces or parks for leisurely walks?
A: Absolutely. Torres De La Alameda has invested in improving its urban green spaces. The &lt;strong&gt;Parque del Calvario&lt;/strong&gt;, with its diverse tree and shrub species and elevated views, is perfect for a leisurely stroll. The &lt;strong&gt;Parque Olof Palme&lt;/strong&gt;, covering about 10,000 square meters, also provides pleasant areas with swings and exercise zones, ideal for relaxation.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Torres De La Alameda?
A: Given the region's characteristics, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Torres De La Alameda. The weather is typically milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions for those who don't mind the chill.

Q: Are there any longer, moderate hikes for experienced walkers?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, the area offers several longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306932319/loeches-from-torres-de-alba-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loeches from Torres de Alba — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.7 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Valdelavieja – Church of Torres de la Alameda loop from Torres de la Alameda&lt;/a&gt; covers 12.6 km, both offering a good workout and varied scenery.

Q: Can I find places to explore beyond just hiking trails?
A: Beyond the hiking trails, the historical context of Torres De La Alameda, with its Roman, Visigothic, and Muslim roots, offers opportunities for cultural exploration. You can visit nearby castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5408145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torremocha&lt;/a&gt; or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt;, which are within reasonable distance and offer a glimpse into the region's heritage.
tips:
A copy, possibly the best in Spain, of the Shroud of Turin is preserved in Torres de la Alameda.
This copy, sanctified by contact with the original in Turin on May 3, 1620, presents –despite the vicissitudes of time– a good state of conservation, and in it the signs of Jesus' suffering can be observed.

The Hermitage of La Soledad was built in the Plateresque style almost simultaneously with the Parish Church of the Asunción, a beautiful Renaissance complex that rises above the rest of the buildings, heralding the name of this unique municipality: Torres de la Alameda.
Road parallel to the M-225, between Torres de Alameda and Valverde de Alcalá. Very quiet environment, with sections between trees, with shade and fields, with views of nearby hills.
Path that takes us towards Cerro de Monte Alto between the fields. Beautiful views and very quiet surroundings. At its end the slope is already quite steep (16-18%).

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and duration of the Torres de Alameda circular route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate hike, requiring good fitness. Despite this, the paths are generally easily accessible and suitable for various skill levels. The estimated duration for completing the 11 km (6.8 miles) route is approximately 3 hours.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse terrain. You'll find approximately 7.34 km of natural path and 2.42 km of unpaved sections. There are also some paved sections (1.18 km) and a small portion of asphalt (150 m). The majority of the route (9.76 km) is dedicated path, with some street (1.18 km) and road (150 m) portions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Torres de Alameda circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll pass by the Church of Torres de la Alameda and the Hermitage of Solitude. The trail also includes sections of the scenic Camino de Los Ángeles and the quiet Path to Cerro de Monte Alto from Torres de Alameda, which winds between fields.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Torres de Alameda circular route?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts and ends in Torres de Alameda. Parking is generally available within the town, near the starting point of the circular route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked or can be found near local amenities.

faqs.question: Is the Torres de Alameda circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or on sections that might have more foot traffic. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter hikes are possible on clear days, though some sections might be muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Torres de Alameda circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect a generally quiet and peaceful environment. The trail offers varied landscapes, winding through sections with trees that provide shade, and open fields that offer expansive views of the surrounding hills and towards Cerro de Monte Alto. The path towards Cerro de Monte Alto is particularly noted for its beautiful and tranquil surroundings.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. Approximately 42% of the route overlaps with the 'Ruta Circular: Sector Nort - Oeste R-C Circular' and about 5% with the 'Senda del Pico del Buho'.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Torres de Alameda, being the starting and ending point of the circular route, offers local amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options within the town to refresh yourself before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and description as 'easily-accessible' for 'all skill levels', the route can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking. The varied terrain and points of interest can keep younger hikers engaged. Always consider your family's fitness level and the total distance.
